message:
fix(suggest): add graceful error handling and recovery for suggestions

Improves the suggest command's resilience by handling partial failures
gracefully, providing useful output even when some operations fail,
and preventing cascading errors from terminating the entire command.

Changes to src/cli/commands/suggest.rs:

Error handling improvements:
- Wrap individual skill scoring in Result handling
- Continue processing remaining skills when one fails
- Collect errors for summary reporting at end
- Log warnings for non-fatal issues

Graceful degradation:
- Return partial results when some scores unavailable
- Fall back to default weights if bandit state missing
- Use cached embeddings if fresh computation fails
- Handle missing metadata without panicking

Recovery mechanisms:
- Retry transient failures (file I/O, embedding)
- Skip corrupted skills rather than aborting
- Initialize missing state files on demand
- Report which skills were skipped and why

Output improvements:
- Always show available suggestions even on partial failure
- Warning count displayed in summary
- Detailed error log in --verbose mode
- Exit code reflects success/partial/failure

Previous behavior:
- Single error would abort entire suggestion flow
- Missing bandit state caused panic
- Corrupted skill file terminated command
- No indication of what went wrong

New behavior:
- Robust operation despite individual failures
- Clear reporting of issues encountered
- Useful suggestions even with problems
- Actionable error messages for debugging

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.5 <noreply@anthropic.com>
